steps.title: What meaning does the blank need?
steps.content: "Peter Edwards has a _____ painting portraits of notable figures." The next sentence calls these "characteristic works" and his portraits are "esteemed." The blank must mean being known for or distinguished by portrait-painting.

steps.title: Substitute each choice
steps.content:
The [phrase] needs to mean that Edwards is known for this type of work. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — complaint about: grievance or objection — Edwards would not be described as having a complaint about his own characteristic, esteemed work. ✗
  • Choice B — reputation for: being widely known for — "Peter Edwards has a reputation for painting portraits" fits: his portraits are esteemed and characteristic. ✓
  • Choice C — question about: uncertainty or doubt — his portraits are described as esteemed and characteristic, not questioned or doubted. ✗
  • Choice D — requirement for: a condition that must be met — painting portraits is not presented as an obligation Edwards must fulfill, but as something he is known for. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
